title: ferray-ufunc: non-contiguous arrays always fall back to scalar iter — no stride-aware kernel
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

helpers.rs has two paths: contiguous (as_slice() succeeds) gets the optimized path; non-contiguous falls back to input.iter().map().collect(). NumPy's ufunc loop machinery (lowlevel_strided_loops) handles arbitrary strides efficiently — it uses the inner-most contiguous dimension for SIMD and strides the outer dimensions. ferray never tries to find the contiguous inner dimension of a strided array. For example, a transposed 2D array has contiguous columns — SIMD could process each column, but ferray just uses the generic iterator. Add stride-aware dispatch that finds the largest contiguous inner loop and applies SIMD to it.
